summary: DHH describes his attempt to improve the audio experience of mobile video by pairing a Nokia 3650 with an iPod. He notes that the phone's mono speaker, designed for phone calls, is inadequate for watching video content. The post is a brief, practical observation from the early days of mobile multimedia convergence.
key_points: Mobile phone speakers in 2003 were designed for voice calls, not media consumption
Early mobile cinema required cobbling together multiple devices for an acceptable experience
The iPod was the obvious solution for better portable audio quality
money_quotes: Mono sound through a loud speaker tuned for low-quality telephone conversations doesn't do much to cooperate the idea of the mobile cinema.
key_insight: In 2003, creating a passable mobile media experience meant combining purpose-built devices since no single gadget could handle both video and decent audio.
